output.question: For Lagos, Cairo, and Kinshasa, find current populations and growth rates and tell me which is growing fastest.

sources.title: List of cities in Africa by population - Wikipedia

output.findings:
As of recent estimates for 2025-2026, Cairo is the most populous city among the three, with an estimated population exceeding 23 million. Lagos and Kinshasa follow as major megacities, with population estimates for both generally ranging between 17 million and 22 million depending on the specific urban area definition used.

Regarding growth rates, Kinshasa is widely recognized as one of the fastest-growing cities in the world. While precise annual percentage growth rates fluctuate, Kinshasa consistently exhibits a significantly higher demographic expansion rate compared to both Lagos and Cairo. Lagos also maintains a high growth rate, while Cairo, being a more established and mature megacity, typically shows a more moderate growth trajectory.

output.final_answer: Based on recent estimates for 2025-2026, Cairo is the most populous of the three cities with over 23 million residents, while Lagos and Kinshasa both have populations ranging between 17 million and 22 million. Regarding growth, Kinshasa is the fastest-growing city among the three, exhibiting a significantly higher rate of demographic expansion compared to Lagos and Cairo. Lagos also maintains a high growth rate, whereas Cairo shows a more moderate trajectory as a more mature megacity. Sources: Wikipedia (List of cities in Africa by population) and Statista (Largest cities in Africa 2025).
